validated_requirement_layer.requirements:
6.1.1. EIM shall create 3 new file layouts for groups, claims, and eligibility to be sent to Intellivo based on the file layouts which can be found in the reference section of this document.
6.1.2. The files shall contain lines of business for Core Fully Insured (CFI), Core Self-Funded (CSF) & Third-Party Administrator (TPA) and FEP claims with the subrogation extended product.
6.1.2.1 The weekly files prior to 07/01/2026 shall be sent based on the current Equian vendor code 'VND 1221' extended product 'Subrogation' setup in FOS for IBC business. For TPA business, the source shall be all TPA groups excluding opt-out customers listed under out of scope. TPA groups on opt out list shall not be sent; all other groups shall be sent (See 6.1.2.3. for additional details).
6.1.2.2. The weekly files post 07/01/2026 shall be sent based on the newly created Intellivo vendor code 'VND-1592' extended product 'Subrogation'. This shall apply to both IBX and TPA lines of business.
6.1.2.3. Third-Party Administrator (TPA) claim data shall include paid claims with paid dates on or after March 1, 2024. Any claims with paid dates prior to March 1, 2024, as well as claims associated with opt-out groups, shall be excluded from all delivered datasets and reporting.
6.1.3. EIM shall create a control file for each of the file layouts showing record counts and totals as needed.
6.1.4. EIM shall create a historical (claim and eligibility file) extract including current NextGen IBC data and FEP data for paid claims for current groups opted in with the Equian vendor code 'VND 1221' extended product 'Subrogation'. TPA groups are not identified by the extended product field. For TPA, Groups on current opt out list in out of scope customer list for subrogation shall not be sent. All other TPA groups shall be sent.
6.1.5. EIM shall pull membership data from DART and correctly identify newly added or updated groups.
6.1.5.1. Groups added in between the monthly file cadence shall be sent on next month's file.
6.1.6. Active IBC CSF & CFI Groups shall be identified by the extended product package dates.
6.1.6.1 IBX shall send paid claims effective from 03/01/2026.
6.1.6.1. Paid claims for term members shall be included if the date of service is within the member's active enrollment period and the group was opted in during that time.
6.1.6.2. Claims with service dates within the opt-in period and active enrollment shall be sent to the vendor.
6.1.6.3. Claims for retroactively termed members shall not be sent to the vendor, and eligibility is determined by group opt-in status and active coverage during the relevant period.
6.1.6.4. Voided claims shall be excluded from all files transmitted to Intellivo.
6.1.6.5. Adjusted claims shall be sent whenever the member or group was in scope, even if the group has since terminated, provided the service period occurred while the member had the subrogation product.
6.1.9.1. When a client terms IBX shall continue to send claims up to that term date based on the Group Package Expiration Date field contained within DART.
6.1.9.2. When a claim is adjusted to deny, it shall not be included on the paid claim file.
6.1.10. CSF, CFI, TPA & FEP data shall be included together within the group, claims, and eligibility files transmitted to Intellivo.
6.1.11. The files shall include fields marked green in the file layout which can also found in the reference section of this document.
6.1.12. Fields listed as "optional" in the File layout shall be sent as blank.
6.1.13. The files shall include Top Account, Subaccount & Benefit Plan ID level data for medical claims only. With the following hierarchy: Customer ID, Group ID, Benefit Plan ID.
6.1.13.1 The Top Account and Subaccount shall be separate fields within the file.
6.1.14. The Account/Group Name field in the Intellivo file spec shall have the value of the Group Name found in DART.
6.1.15. The Account/Group ID field in the Intellivo file spec shall have the value of the Group ID found in DART.
6.1.16. The Legal Plan Name field in the Intellivo file spec shall have the value of the IBC Level Code and Rollup Description found in DART.
6.1.17. The Life Count field in the Intellivo file spec shall have the value of the total members found in DART.
6.1.18. The Company ID field in the Intellivo file spec shall have the value of CID number Found in DART.
6.1.19. The Member Number field in the Intellivo file shall contain the Member ID from DART, including the alphanumeric prefix. The member key field shall be blank.
6.1.20. The Subscriber Number field in the Intellivo file spec shall have the value of the Subscription ID found in DART.
6.1.21. The “Check Number” field in the Intellivo File spec on the claim data specifications shall be blank.
6.1.22. The “Intellivo Fee” field in the file spec on the group information specifications shall be blank.
6.1.23. The “Client Fee” field in the file spec on the group information specifications shall be blank.
6.1.23.1 The Amount Denied, Amount Capitated & Amount allowed field shall be blank.
6.1.24.2 The Rendering Provider field shall be populated by concatenating the first name and last name of the associated practitioner or service provider.
6.1.24. The Social Security Number (SSN) shall only be included on the eligibility file and include the last four digits.
6.1.25. EIM shall include Custom Column 1 on the claims file for a claim line status code field.
6.1.26. The outbound "Claims" file shall include a field that indicates the applicable platforms: Core Fully Insured (CFI), Core Self-Funded (CSF) & Federal Employee Program (FEP).
6.1.27. IBX shall send paid claims files at the time when a group is still active.
6.2.12. DART shall source the Product Option choices data from FOS.
6.2.13. DART shall make the Product Option choices data available for downstream users.
6.2.14. DART shall use the Intellivo vendor option to derive the values needed for use in the Intellivo group, claims and eligibility files.

filters_and_parameters.company: Independence Blue Cross (including Quality Care Company (QCC), Keystone Health Plan East (KHPE), AmeriHealth PA (AHPA), AmeriHealth Insurance of New Jersey (AHNJ)), Third Party Administrators (TPA) (including AmeriHealth Administrators (AHA), Independence Administrators (IA))

filters_and_parameters.financial_arrangement: Fully Insured, Self-Funded (Mixed Funded, Fixed Funded)
filters_and_parameters.claim_status: Paid. Voided claims shall be excluded. When a payment is initially paid but then denied, that claim will be sent.
filters_and_parameters.excluded_companies: Independence Assurance Company (IAC), Independence Hospital Indemnity Plan Incorporated, AmeriHealth Administrators, Incorporated (GEOBlue – Blue Cross Global Travel Insurance Solutions, Blue Link Third Party Administrators (TPA))

date_parameters.member_active_enrollment: Paid claims for term members shall be included if the date of service is within the member's active enrollment period and the group was opted in during that time.
date_parameters.active_plan_group: Active IBC CSF & CFI Groups shall be identified by the extended product package dates.
date_parameters.start_date: TPA claim data: on or after March 1, 2024. IBX paid claims: effective from 03/01/2026.
date_parameters.history_lookback: Historical (claim and eligibility file) extract including current NextGen IBC data and FEP data for paid claims for current groups opted in with the Equian vendor code 'VND 1221' extended product 'Subrogation'.
date_parameters.claim_service_dates: Claims with service dates within the opt-in period and active enrollment shall be sent to the vendor.
date_parameters.paid_dates: TPA claim data shall include paid claims with paid dates on or after March 1, 2024. IBX shall send paid claims effective from 03/01/2026.
date_parameters.pharmacy_fill_dates: Pharmacy claims will be supplied by the Subrogation team via report when requested. No EIM export will be generated for these claims.
date_parameters.pharmacy_cut_dates: Pharmacy claims will be supplied by the Subrogation team via report when requested. No EIM export will be generated for these claims.

file_attributes_mapping.file_type: ASCII pipe-delimited (.txt) format with Header Rows
file_attributes_mapping.control_file_required: Yes, for each of the file layouts showing record counts and totals as needed.
file_attributes_mapping.field_requirements: The files shall include fields marked green in the file layout. Fields listed as "optional" in the File layout shall be sent as blank.
file_attributes_mapping.default_values: Fields listed as 'optional' in the File layout shall be sent as blank. The 'Check Number', 'Intellivo Fee', 'Client Fee', 'Amount Denied', 'Amount Capitated', and 'Amount allowed' fields shall be blank.
file_attributes_mapping.data_format_rules: The Social Security Number (SSN) shall only be included on the eligibility file and include the last four digits. The Rendering Provider field shall be populated by concatenating the first name and last name of the associated practitioner or service provider.

file_specs.physical_file_name: IBX_Intellivo_Group_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.txt, IBX_Intellivo_Member_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.txt, IBX_Intellivo_Claims_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.txt and TEST_ versions.
file_specs.dependencies:
| Title | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1. Secure File Transfer Protocol (SFTP) | A New SFTP connection between IBX and Intellivo will be created. IBX to send group, claims and elgigbility files to Intellivo as produced. |
file_specs.email_notification_dl: File Delivery Receipt: Maureen.rutledge@ibx.com, Nykeemah.Payne@ibx.com. File Issue: Maureen.rutledge@ibx.com, Nykeemah.Payne@ibx.com, obdf@ibx.com. 762 Remittance Report: finfinancialdevelopment@ibx.com, Maureen.rutledge@ibx.com, Nykeemah.Payne@ibx.com, Kimberly.ward@ibx.com.
file_specs.file_population_type: Group & Eligibility: Full file. Claims: Incremental file.
file_specs.assumptions:
| Title | Description |
| --- | --- |
| 1. Data Mapping | All required fields like Group ID, sub-account, top account, etc. will be mapped reliably from DART to Intellivo's required file layout. |
| 2. Health Rules Payer (HRP) | There will be no changes to HRP for this effort. |
| 3. Product Updates | No product changes are required. The existing population of clients using Optum as their subrogation vendor will continue to be included in the files sent to Intellivo. Clients will not have the option to choose between subrogation vendors, as only one subrogation product will be available. |
| 4. Optum | No updates are needed to Optum's existing data feeds. The second-pass timer will be configured directly by Optum and does not require any file changes from IBX. |
| 5. Finance/Claims Adjustment | Successful subrogation cases will continue to follow the current finance and claims adjustment processes without modification. |
| 6. Accounting Setup | Accounting will confirm setup of the new vendor and will include Intellivo in GL reconciliation purposes for subrogation payments. |
| 7. Statistical Adjustment | The statistical adjustment process used to apply subrogation payments to claims will remain unchanged. |
| 8. EOB/EOP | No changes will be made to Explanation of Benefits (EOB) or Explanation of Payment (EOP) documents. |
| 9. Accumulators | Accumulators will continue to be updated through the existing statistical adjustment process. |
| 10. Fee Fields | Fee fields populated through the statistical adjustment process will continue to feed into CABS and will be billed to the SF customer as they are today. Finance will continue to populate all required information using current file layouts. |
| 11. Subrogation Cases | Conflicts between vendors regarding subrogation cases will be managed and resolved by the Subrogation team. Any updates resulting from these resolutions will be submitted as tickets to the appropriate teams. |
| 12. Provider Disbursement | Provider disbursements will not be impacted, as payments received through the subrogation process are not forwarded to providers. |
| 13. Pharmacy Report | Pharmacy claims will be supplied by the Subrogation team via report when requested. No EIM export will be generated for these claims. |
| 14. CFMS | Payments received from the vendor will be added to CFMS using the current processes. |
| 15. Adjustment Files | Adjustment files from Finance that contain Intellivo payment adjustments can be processed using the same approach currently used for Optum adjustment files. No layout changes are required. |
| 16. Payments | Payments received from Intellivo will be included in manually created adjustment files that are sent to CFI, CSF and FEP. |
file_specs.control_file_name: IBX_Intellivo_Group_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.ctl, IBX_Intellivo_Member_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.ctl, IBX_Intellivo_Claims_YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.ctl, and TEST_ versions.

common_rules.incremental_history_required: Yes, the claims file is incremental.
common_rules.runout_required: Yes, when a client terms IBX shall continue to send claims up to that term date.
common_rules.deidentity_extract: Yes, the Social Security Number (SSN) shall only be included on the eligibility file and include the last four digits.

message: Validation complete. All fields verified — no corrections needed.
